summary: Building 'Concept Delta' – a responsive MHT-CET Test Series website with Navy Blue theme. Includes Home Page, Dashboard, Test Page, and Admin Panel. Features Google/Internet Identity auth with profile setup (name, class, contact), Motoko backend with users/questions/tests/results data models, image-based questions where each question has one single image containing the full question and all 4 options (A, B, C, D), timed auto-submit tests, and an admin panel for question/test management and user/result viewing. Questions use a single uploaded image per question (no separate option images); answer selection is via four text buttons (A, B, C, D) shown below the question image. Images stored as base64 or via asset upload in the backend. Admin uploads images directly from device gallery.
architectural_notes: Motoko backend on Internet Computer with stable storage for Users, Questions, Tests, TestResults
React/TypeScript frontend with Tailwind CSS, Navy Blue (#0D1B4B) primary color, white background
Internet Identity (passkey/Google-style) for authentication; admin protected by principal check
Mobile-first responsive design with hamburger menu on mobile
Navbar links: Home (all), Dashboard (authenticated), Admin (admin only). My Results and Leaderboard removed from navbar.
First principal to authenticate is auto-designated as admin; admin role stored in stable backend state
Each question uses a single uploaded image containing the full question and all 4 options; answer selection is via A/B/C/D text buttons (no separate option images displayed on test screen)
known_issues: Admin Panel link not appearing in navbar for admin users
Question image on test screen is too small; A/B/C/D buttons take too much space, reducing image visibility on mobile
Navbar intermittently hides Home, Dashboard, Admin Panel, or Login/Logout buttons

existing_features.title: Build a Home Page with: a sticky navbar containing a triangular delta SVG logo and the text 'Concept Delta' on the left, and a Login/Connect button on the right using Internet Identity; a hero section with the heading 'Free MHT-CET Mock Tests', subheading 'Created by COEPians – COEP Technological University, one of the top engineering colleges in Maharashtra', motivational student-focused body copy about smart practice and free mock tests, and a large CTA button 'Start Practicing Now' that triggers login if unauthenticated or navigates to Dashboard if authenticated; social buttons for Telegram (https://t.me/Conceptdelta) and YouTube (https://youtube.com/@conceptdelta2026) below the CTA.

Implement an authentication and profile setup flow: after a user logs in with Internet Identity for the first time (isProfileComplete returns false), show a full-screen profile setup modal/page asking for Full Name (text input), Class (dropdown: 11th / 12th / Dropper), and Contact Number (tel input). On submission, call saveUserProfile and then redirect to Dashboard. Skip the modal for returning users with a complete profile.

Build a Dashboard page (protected, requires login + complete profile) that fetches all published tests via getPublishedTests() and renders each as a card showing: test name, formatted duration (e.g. '30 min'), and a 'Start Test' button that navigates to the Test Page for that test. Show a loading skeleton while fetching and an empty state if no tests are published.

Build a Test Page that receives a testId, fetches questions via getTestQuestions, and renders: a countdown timer at the top showing remaining time (mm:ss), the current question's image, four option images displayed as clickable cards (visually highlighted on selection), Previous/Next navigation buttons, and a Submit button. When the timer reaches zero or Submit is clicked, call submitTest with the collected answers array, then show a result screen displaying 'Test Submitted!' and the score (e.g. '18 / 30 correct').

Build an Admin Panel page (protected, accessible only to the designated admin principal) with four tabs: (1) Question Gallery – form to upload question image URL and four option image URLs, select correct option (radio), and submit via addQuestion; displays all existing questions in a grid with images. (2) Test Builder – form to enter test name and duration, select questions from gallery via checkboxes, and create test via createTest; lists all tests with a publish/unpublish toggle per test using setTestPublished. (3) User List – table showing all users' full name, class, contact number, and principal. (4) Results – table showing username, test name, and score for all submissions.

Apply a consistent Navy Blue (#0D1B4B) and white design theme across all pages: Navy Blue for primary buttons, navbar background, and accent elements; white for page backgrounds and card surfaces; clean sans-serif typography; modern minimal card-based layout; fully mobile-responsive with a hamburger menu on small screens; smooth hover and focus states on interactive elements.

Add a 'My Results' page (route: /results) accessible from the Dashboard navbar. It should fetch the current user's past test submissions and display them in a list/table showing: test name, score (e.g. '18 / 30'), percentage, and submission date/time. Show a loading skeleton while fetching and an empty state message if no results exist yet.

On the Test result screen (after submission), display a detailed answer review section below the score. For each question, show: the question image, all four option images, highlight the user's selected answer (e.g. blue border), and highlight the correct answer (e.g. green border). Include a 'Back to Dashboard' button.

Add a question count badge and subject/topic tag field to the Admin Panel Test Builder. When creating a test, allow the admin to optionally enter a subject label (e.g. 'Physics', 'Chemistry', 'Mathematics'). Store this label in the Test data model. Display the subject label and total question count on each test card on the Dashboard.

Add a leaderboard tab to the Admin Panel (5th tab: 'Leaderboard') that shows the top performers across all tests. Display a ranked table with columns: Rank, Student Name, Total Tests Taken, Average Score (%), and Best Score. Derive this data from existing TestResult and UserProfile records on the backend via a new `getLeaderboard() → [LeaderboardEntry]` function.

Add a progress/stats summary section at the top of the Dashboard (above the test cards) showing the logged-in student's personal stats: total tests attempted, average score percentage, and best score percentage. Derive these from the student's own past results using the existing getMyResults hook.

Add a public-facing Leaderboard page (route: /leaderboard) accessible to all logged-in students from the Dashboard navbar. Fetch data via the existing getLeaderboard() backend function and display a ranked table with columns: Rank, Student Name, Total Tests Taken, Average Score (%), and Best Score. Show a loading skeleton while fetching and an empty state if no data exists.
Add a 'Leaderboard' navigation link in the Navbar component (alongside Dashboard and My Results) so authenticated students can navigate to /leaderboard from any page.
Register the /leaderboard route in the TanStack Router configuration in App.tsx, pointing to the new Leaderboard page component.
Delete the public-facing Leaderboard page component at frontend/src/pages/Leaderboard.tsx.
Remove the /leaderboard route registration from the TanStack Router configuration in frontend/src/App.tsx so the route no longer exists.
Remove the 'Leaderboard' navigation link from the Navbar component (frontend/src/components/Navbar.tsx) so it no longer appears in the authenticated navigation bar.

In the Admin Panel Question Gallery tab (frontend/src/components/admin/QuestionGallery.tsx), replace any existing inline add-question form with an 'Add Question' button. When clicked, open a modal dialog containing: a question image URL input field, four option image URL input fields (Option 1–4), a correct option selector (radio buttons or tick/checkmark icons for each option), and a Submit button. On submit, call the addQuestion backend function with the provided values, close the modal on success, show a success toast notification, and refresh the question gallery grid to display the newly added question.

In frontend/src/components/Navbar.tsx, fix the Admin Panel navigation link so it correctly appears for authenticated users who are admins. The link should navigate to /admin and be conditionally rendered only when the user is logged in AND the useIsAdmin (or equivalent) query returns true. Ensure the admin check hook is properly called and its result is used to gate the link rendering.

In frontend/src/components/Navbar.tsx, update the navigation links so that only three links are shown: 'Home' (always visible), 'Dashboard' (visible when authenticated), and 'Admin Panel' (visible only when authenticated AND useIsCallerAdmin returns true). Remove 'My Results' and any other links (e.g. Leaderboard) from the navbar entirely. The mobile hamburger menu must reflect the same conditional link set.

In frontend/src/pages/Admin.tsx, ensure the Admin Panel contains all data-management tabs: (1) Question Gallery, (2) Test Builder, (3) User List, (4) Results, and (5) Leaderboard. These tabs must remain fully functional and accessible only to authenticated admin users. No data-management UI should exist outside the Admin Panel.

In frontend/src/pages/Dashboard.tsx, ensure the Dashboard page only shows the student-facing content: the personal stats summary section and the list of published test cards (fetched via getPublishedTests). Remove any admin-facing controls or data tables (questions, users, results) from the Dashboard. The 'My Results' page at /results must remain accessible by direct URL navigation even though its link is removed from the navbar.

In frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts, ensure the `useIsCallerAdmin` query correctly calls the backend `isCallerAdmin()` function and returns its boolean result. The query must re-fetch when the authenticated identity changes so the Admin Panel link appears immediately after the first-login admin logs in.

In frontend/src/components/Navbar.tsx, confirm that the 'Admin Panel' navigation link (navigating to /admin) is conditionally rendered only when the user is authenticated AND useIsCallerAdmin returns true. The link must not appear for any non-admin authenticated user. This applies to both the desktop nav and the mobile hamburger menu.

In frontend/src/pages/Admin.tsx, confirm that the Admin Panel page access guard redirects or shows an 'Access Denied' message for any authenticated user who is not the designated admin. The guard must use the result of useIsCallerAdmin and must not rely solely on client-side state — admin-only backend calls will naturally be rejected server-side as well.

Update the Admin Panel 'Add Question' modal (frontend/src/components/admin/AddQuestionModal.tsx) to replace image URL input fields with plain text input fields. The modal must contain: a multi-line textarea for the question text, four text input fields labeled 'Option 1' through 'Option 4', a correct option selector (radio buttons), and a Submit button. On submit, call the updated addQuestion backend function with the text values.

Update the Admin Panel Question Gallery (frontend/src/components/admin/QuestionGallery.tsx) to display questions and options as text instead of rendering images. Each question card must show the full question text prominently, and list all four option texts with the correct answer clearly labelled.

Update the QuestionDisplay component (frontend/src/components/QuestionDisplay.tsx) to render questions and options as text instead of images. Display the question text in a readable, responsive typographic style. Display each of the four options as a selectable card containing the option text. The layout must be responsive: on mobile screens, options stack in a single column; on tablet/desktop screens, options are arranged in a 2-column grid. Selected option is highlighted with a navy border and label badge as before.

Update the Test Page answer review section (frontend/src/pages/Test.tsx) to show question text and option texts instead of question/option images. For each reviewed question, display the question text, all four option texts as styled cards, highlight the user's selected answer with a blue border, and highlight the correct answer with a green border. The layout must be responsive: single-column on mobile, 2-column option grid on desktop.

Update the Admin Panel 'Add Question' modal (frontend/src/components/admin/AddQuestionModal.tsx) to use image URL input fields instead of plain text fields. The modal must contain: a text input for the question image URL, four text inputs labeled 'Option 1 Image URL' through 'Option 4 Image URL', a correct option selector (radio buttons), and a Submit button. On submit, call the addQuestion backend function with the image URL values. Show a preview thumbnail (small, e.g. max-h-32) below each URL input field so the admin can verify the image before submitting.

Update the Admin Panel Question Gallery (frontend/src/components/admin/QuestionGallery.tsx) to display questions and options as images instead of text. Each question card must render the question image prominently, and show all four option images in a 2×2 grid below it, with the correct answer option visually highlighted (e.g. green border). Image containers must be responsive: use `w-full object-contain` with a defined max height (e.g. max-h-48 for question, max-h-24 for options) so images scale to fit without overflow, and are clearly visible on both mobile and desktop.

Update the QuestionDisplay component (frontend/src/components/QuestionDisplay.tsx) to render the question and answer options as images instead of text. Display the question image in a responsive container (w-full, object-contain, adjustable max height e.g. max-h-64 on mobile, max-h-80 on desktop) so it is clearly visible. Display each of the four option images as a selectable card in a 2-column grid on tablet/desktop and single column on mobile. Each option image container must use object-contain with a defined max height (e.g. max-h-40) so images are fully visible. The selected option card is highlighted with a navy border.

Update the Test Page answer review section (frontend/src/pages/Test.tsx) to render question and option images instead of text. For each reviewed question, display the question image in a responsive container (object-contain, max-h-64). Show all four option images as styled cards in a 2-column grid on desktop and single column on mobile, each with object-contain and max-h-40. Highlight the user's selected answer card with a blue border and the correct answer card with a green border.

Update the Admin Panel 'Add Question' modal (frontend/src/components/admin/AddQuestionModal.tsx) to replace all image URL text inputs with file picker inputs (type='file', accept='image/*') that allow the admin to upload images directly from their device gallery. There must be one file picker for the question image and four file pickers for option images (Option 1–4). When a file is selected, read it as a base64 data URI using FileReader and store it in component state. Show a live preview thumbnail (max-h-32, object-contain, w-full) below each file picker so the admin can verify the image before submitting. On submit, call the addQuestion backend function with the base64 data URI strings. Close the modal on success, show a success toast, and refresh the question gallery.

Update the Admin Panel Question Gallery (frontend/src/components/admin/QuestionGallery.tsx) to render question and option images from base64 data URIs returned by the backend. Each question card must display the question image in a responsive container using w-full, object-contain, and a max-height that adjusts to the natural aspect ratio of the image (use max-h-48 as default, allow vertical scroll within the container if image is taller). Show all four option images in a 2×2 grid below the question image, each in an object-contain container with max-h-24 and a defined min-height so small images are still clearly visible. Highlight the correct answer option card with a green border. All image containers must be clearly visible and non-cropped on both mobile and desktop.

Update the QuestionDisplay component (frontend/src/components/QuestionDisplay.tsx) to render the question image and four option images from base64 data URIs. The question image container must use w-full, object-contain, and an adaptive max-height: max-h-56 on mobile, max-h-80 on tablet/desktop (using responsive Tailwind classes), so images of varying sizes are fully visible without cropping. Each of the four option images must be displayed as a selectable card in a 2-column grid on tablet/desktop and single column on mobile. Option image containers must use object-contain with max-h-40 and a min-height so small images are still clearly visible. The selected option card is highlighted with a navy border and option label badge (A–D) as before.

Update the Test Page answer review section (frontend/src/pages/Test.tsx) to render question and option images from base64 data URIs. For each reviewed question, display the question image in a responsive container with object-contain and max-h-64 (adaptive: max-h-48 on mobile, max-h-64 on desktop). Show all four option images as styled cards in a 2-column grid on desktop and single column on mobile, each with object-contain and max-h-40. Highlight the user's selected answer card with a blue border and the correct answer card with a green border. Image containers must be adjustable and clearly display images of varying sizes.

Update the QuestionDisplay component (frontend/src/components/QuestionDisplay.tsx) to show only a single question image per question — remove all four separate option image blocks (A, B, C, D image cards). The question image must use w-full, object-contain, be centered, maintain aspect ratio, have a white background container, and have a max-height of approximately 70–75vh on mobile (e.g. `max-h-[70vh] md:max-h-[75vh]`). The image container must be vertically scrollable (overflow-y-auto) if the image height exceeds the viewport. Below the image, render four full-width text buttons labeled 'A', 'B', 'C', 'D' stacked vertically with consistent spacing between them. Each button must have a minimum height of 48–56px (e.g. `min-h-[52px]`). The selected button must be highlighted with a navy border and a light navy background change to indicate selection. Clicking a button stores the selected option (0–3) in the parent-provided state via the existing onSelect callback. No option images are rendered anywhere in this component.

Update the Test Page (frontend/src/pages/Test.tsx) answer review section (shown after test submission) to reflect the new single-image layout: for each reviewed question, display only the question image (w-full, object-contain, max-h-[48vh] on mobile, max-h-64 on desktop, white background container). Remove all option image rendering from the review section. Below each question image, show four full-width text cards/buttons labeled 'A', 'B', 'C', 'D'. Highlight the card matching the user's selected answer with a blue border. Highlight the card matching the correct answer with a green border. If both are the same, show the green border. Timer, question navigation, submission logic, and the 'Back to Dashboard' button must remain completely unchanged.

In the Admin Panel 'Add Question' modal (frontend/src/components/admin/AddQuestionModal.tsx), remove all four option image file picker inputs (Option 1–4). The modal must retain only: the single question image file picker (with live preview thumbnail), the correct option selector (radio buttons for A, B, C, D), and the Submit button. On submit, call the addQuestion backend function passing the single question image base64 data URI and empty strings (or a placeholder) for the four option image data fields to maintain backend compatibility. Close the modal on success, show a success toast, and refresh the question gallery.

In frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts, fix the `useIsCallerAdmin` query so it: (1) is enabled only when the user is authenticated (identity is available), (2) uses the authenticated actor (not anonymous) to call the backend `isCallerAdmin()` function, (3) includes the identity/principal as a query key dependency so it re-fetches automatically when the user logs in or out, and (4) has a short staleTime (e.g. 0 or 5000ms) so it re-fetches on mount after login.

In frontend/src/components/Navbar.tsx, fix the Admin Panel link visibility: (1) call `useIsCallerAdmin` at the top of the component, (2) after login completes, ensure the query result is awaited before deciding whether to show the link, (3) conditionally render the 'Admin Panel' link (navigating to /admin) in both the desktop nav and mobile hamburger menu only when `isLoggedIn === true` AND `isCallerAdmin === true`, (4) do not render the link during the loading state of the admin check to avoid flicker.

In frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts, fix the `useIsCallerAdmin` query so it: (1) is enabled only when the user is authenticated (identity is non-null), (2) uses the authenticated actor to call the backend `isCallerAdmin()` function, (3) includes the identity principal string as a query key dependency so it re-fetches automatically when the user logs in or out, and (4) has staleTime set to 0 so it re-fetches on every mount after login.

In frontend/src/components/Navbar.tsx, fix the Admin Panel link so it correctly appears for authenticated admin users: (1) call `useIsCallerAdmin` at the top level of the component, (2) conditionally render the 'Admin Panel' link (navigating to /admin) in both the desktop nav and the mobile hamburger menu only when the user is authenticated AND `isCallerAdmin` is true, (3) do not render the link while the admin check query is in a loading state to prevent flicker, (4) do not render the link for any non-admin authenticated user.

In frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts, rewrite the useIsCallerAdmin query so that: (1) it is enabled only when the user is authenticated (identity is non-null), (2) it uses the authenticated actor to call the backend isCallerAdmin() function, (3) its query key includes the identity principal string as a dependency so it automatically re-fetches when the user logs in or out, and (4) staleTime is set to 0 so it re-fetches on every component mount after login.

In frontend/src/components/Navbar.tsx, fix the Admin Panel link so it reliably appears for authenticated admin users: (1) call useIsCallerAdmin at the top level of the Navbar component, (2) conditionally render the 'Admin Panel' link (navigating to /admin) in both the desktop nav and the mobile hamburger menu only when the user is authenticated AND isCallerAdmin is true, (3) do not render the link while the admin check query is in a loading state to avoid flicker, (4) do not render the link for any non-admin authenticated user.

In frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts, rewrite the useIsCallerAdmin query so that: (1) it is enabled only when the user is authenticated (identity is non-null), (2) it uses the authenticated actor — not the anonymous actor — to call the backend isCallerAdmin() function, (3) its query key includes the identity principal string as a dependency so it automatically re-fetches when the user logs in or out, and (4) staleTime is set to 0 so it re-fetches on every component mount after login.

In frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts, rewrite the useIsCallerAdmin query with a completely fresh approach: (1) obtain the identity directly from useInternetIdentity, (2) derive a stable principal string from the identity, (3) set enabled to true only when identity is non-null, (4) use the authenticated actor — obtained via a direct createActor call with the identity rather than relying on the cached actor — to call isCallerAdmin(), (5) include the principal string in the query key, and (6) set staleTime to 0 and gcTime to 0 so the result is never stale.

In frontend/src/components/Navbar.tsx, overhaul the Admin Panel link rendering: (1) call useIsCallerAdmin and destructure its data, isLoading, and isFetching states, (2) show the Admin Panel link only when isLoggedIn is true AND isLoading is false AND isFetching is false AND the returned value is strictly true, (3) add a console.log or debug statement (to be removed later) logging the isCallerAdmin result and loading state so the issue can be diagnosed, (4) ensure the same conditional logic applies to both the desktop nav and the mobile hamburger menu.

Update the QuestionDisplay component (frontend/src/components/QuestionDisplay.tsx) to make the question image container full-screen height on mobile. The image container must use w-full, object-contain, be centered, maintain aspect ratio, have a white background, and occupy the full available viewport height minus the navbar and the answer buttons row (e.g. use `h-[calc(100vh-180px)]` or equivalent so the image fills as much screen space as possible). The image container must be vertically scrollable (overflow-y-auto) if the image is taller than the container. Below the image, render four compact inline/row-arranged text buttons labeled 'A', 'B', 'C', 'D' displayed horizontally in a single row (not stacked full-width). Each button must be small and compact (e.g. w-12 h-12 or similar square size, rounded) with just the letter label centered inside. The selected button must be highlighted with a navy border and a light navy background. Clicking a button stores the selected option (0–3) via the existing onSelect callback. No option images are rendered anywhere in this component.

Update the Test Page answer review section (frontend/src/pages/Test.tsx) to match the new layout: for each reviewed question, display the question image in a large responsive container (w-full, object-contain, h-[calc(100vh-220px)] or similar tall height, white background, overflow-y-auto). Below the question image, show four compact square inline buttons labeled 'A', 'B', 'C', 'D' in a single horizontal row. Highlight the button matching the user's selected answer with a blue border and light blue background. Highlight the button matching the correct answer with a green border and light green background. If both are the same, show green. Timer, question navigation, submission logic, and the 'Back to Dashboard' button must remain completely unchanged.

In frontend/src/components/Navbar.tsx, update the navigation links so that exactly three links are shown: 'Home' (always visible, navigates to /), 'Dashboard' (visible only when the user is authenticated, navigates to /dashboard), and 'Admin Panel' (visible only when the user is authenticated AND useIsCallerAdmin returns true, navigates to /admin). Remove the 'My Results' link entirely from both the desktop nav and the mobile hamburger menu. No other nav links should be present. The mobile hamburger menu must reflect this same conditional link set.

In frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts, completely rewrite the useIsCallerAdmin query using a self-contained approach: (1) import useInternetIdentity directly and obtain the identity object, (2) derive a stable principalString from identity?.getPrincipal().toText() ?? '', (3) set enabled: !!identity so the query only runs when authenticated, (4) inside the queryFn, create a fresh authenticated actor by calling createActor with the identity (do not rely on any cached actor from useActor), (5) call isCallerAdmin() on that fresh actor and return the boolean result, (6) use ['isCallerAdmin', principalString] as the query key so it re-fetches whenever the logged-in principal changes, and (7) set both staleTime: 0 and gcTime: 0 to ensure the result is never served from cache.

In frontend/src/components/Navbar.tsx, fix the Admin Panel link rendering with the following approach: (1) call useIsCallerAdmin at the top level of the component and destructure data, isLoading, and isFetching, (2) derive a boolean isAdmin = data === true, (3) render the 'Admin Panel' link (navigating to /admin) in BOTH the desktop nav and the mobile hamburger menu only when isLoggedIn === true AND isLoading === false AND isFetching === false AND isAdmin === true, (4) do not render the link while the query is loading or fetching to avoid flicker, (5) remove any console.log or debug statements that were added in earlier iterations. The three nav links must be: 'Home' (always visible), 'Dashboard' (authenticated only), 'Admin Panel' (authenticated + isAdmin only).

Update the QuestionDisplay component (frontend/src/components/QuestionDisplay.tsx) so the question image occupies the maximum available screen height. The image container must use w-full, object-contain, be centered, have a white background, and use h-[calc(100vh-140px)] so the image fills nearly the entire viewport (minus navbar and the compact button row). The container must be vertically scrollable (overflow-y-auto) if the image exceeds container height. Below the image, render exactly four compact square buttons labeled 'A', 'B', 'C', 'D' arranged in a single horizontal row centered on screen. Each button must be small and square: w-12 h-12 (48×48px), rounded-lg, with the letter centered in bold. The selected button must be highlighted with a navy (#0D1B4B) border and a light navy background (e.g. bg-blue-50 border-2 border-[#0D1B4B]). Unselected buttons use a neutral border. Clicking a button stores the selected option index (0–3) via the existing onSelect callback. No option images are rendered anywhere in this component. The total height of the button row must not exceed 60px so the image area is maximized.

Update the Test Page answer review section (frontend/src/pages/Test.tsx) to match the new compact button layout. For each reviewed question, display the question image in a large responsive container (w-full, object-contain, h-[calc(100vh-200px)], white background, overflow-y-auto) so the image is maximized. Below each question image, show four compact square inline buttons labeled 'A', 'B', 'C', 'D' in a single horizontal row (same sizing as QuestionDisplay: w-12 h-12, rounded-lg). Highlight the button matching the user's selected answer with a blue border and light blue background. Highlight the button matching the correct answer with a green border and light green background. If the selected and correct answers are the same, show green only. Remove any large option image cards or full-width stacked buttons from the review section. Timer, question navigation, submission logic, and the 'Back to Dashboard' button must remain completely unchanged.

Rewrite frontend/src/components/Navbar.tsx so that all navigation elements are always rendered and never hidden due to layout or overflow issues. The navbar must use a flex layout with `flex-nowrap` and `overflow-visible` on the desktop nav container so items are never clipped or collapsed. The three nav links must be: 'Home' (always visible, navigates to /), 'Dashboard' (visible only when the user is authenticated, navigates to /dashboard), and 'Admin Panel' (visible only when the user is authenticated AND isAdmin is true, navigates to /admin). The Login/Logout button must always be visible on the right side of the navbar, never wrapped or hidden. On desktop (md and above), all visible links and the Login/Logout button must appear in a single horizontal row without overflow. On mobile, the hamburger menu must include the same conditional link set (Home always, Dashboard if authenticated, Admin Panel if authenticated + isAdmin) plus the Login/Logout action. The mobile menu must open and close reliably with no z-index or overflow conflicts.

Rewrite frontend/src/components/Navbar.tsx with a fully opaque navy (#0D1B4B) background that is never transparent, even when rendered over a dark hero image. The navbar must have a solid background-color at all times — do not use bg-transparent, bg-opacity-*, or any backdrop-blur that could cause the navbar content to visually disappear against dark page backgrounds. Set an explicit z-index (e.g. z-50) so the navbar always renders above page content.

In frontend/src/components/Navbar.tsx, ensure all navigation links and the Login/Logout button are always visible and never clipped or hidden. The desktop nav must use a flex layout with flex-nowrap and sufficient horizontal spacing so all rendered items fit in a single row. The three conditional nav links must be: 'Home' (always visible, navigates to /), 'Dashboard' (visible only when authenticated, navigates to /dashboard), and 'Admin Panel' (visible only when authenticated AND isAdmin is true, navigates to /admin). The Login/Logout button must always appear on the far right. No link or button must overflow, wrap, or be hidden at any viewport width on desktop (md and above).

In frontend/src/components/Navbar.tsx, fix the mobile hamburger menu to correctly display all conditional links against the opaque navy background. The mobile dropdown menu must have a solid opaque navy (#0D1B4B) background (not transparent) so links are readable. Include the same conditional link set as desktop: 'Home' (always), 'Dashboard' (authenticated only), 'Admin Panel' (authenticated + isAdmin only), plus the Login/Logout action. The hamburger icon and the mobile dropdown must be clearly visible on all page backgrounds including dark hero images.

In frontend/src/components/Navbar.tsx, ensure the useIsCallerAdmin query is called at the top level of the component. Derive isAdmin = data === true. Render the 'Admin Panel' link in both desktop nav and mobile hamburger menu only when the user is authenticated AND isAdmin === true AND the query is not in a loading/fetching state. Remove any console.log or debug statements from prior iterations. The three nav links must be exactly: 'Home' (always visible), 'Dashboard' (authenticated only), 'Admin Panel' (authenticated + isAdmin only).

new_feature_requests.summary: Home Page with navbar, delta logo, hero section, CTA, Telegram and YouTube buttons
Google/Internet Identity authentication with profile setup (name, class, contact)
Dashboard showing published test cards with name, duration, and Start Test button
Test Page with question image, 4 option images, countdown timer, auto-submit, and score display
Admin Panel with question gallery, test builder, publish toggle, user list, and results view
Motoko backend with Users, Questions, Tests, TestResults data models and CRUD functions

Create a Motoko backend with stable storage for four data models: Users (principal, fullName, class: {#eleventh; #twelfth; #dropper}, contactNumber, registeredAt), Questions (id, questionImageUrl, optionImageUrls: [Text; 4], correctOption: Nat, createdAt), Tests (id, name, durationSeconds: Nat, questionIds: [Text], isPublished: Bool, createdAt), and TestResults (id, userId: Principal, testId: Text, answers: [Nat], score: Nat, submittedAt).

Implement backend functions: saveUserProfile(fullName: Text, class: UserClass, contactNumber: Text) saves or updates the caller's profile; getUserProfile() returns the caller's profile or null; isProfileComplete() returns Bool indicating whether the caller has completed profile setup.

Implement admin-only backend functions: addQuestion(questionImageUrl: Text, optionImageUrls: [Text], correctOption: Nat) → Text (returns question id); getAllQuestions() → [Question]; createTest(name: Text, durationSeconds: Nat, questionIds: [Text]) → Text (returns test id); setTestPublished(testId: Text, published: Bool); getAllUsers() → [UserProfile]; getAllResults() → [TestResult]. The first principal to call any admin function is designated the admin; subsequent admin calls from other principals are rejected.

Implement test-taking backend functions: getPublishedTests() → [TestSummary] returns name, id, durationSeconds for all published tests; getTestQuestions(testId: Text) → [Question] returns questions for a test; submitTest(testId: Text, answers: [Nat]) → Nat calculates and stores score (number of correct answers), saves a TestResult record, and returns the score.
Add a backend function `getMyResults() → [TestResult]` that returns all TestResult records belonging to the calling principal, sorted by submittedAt descending.

In Admin Panel Question Gallery, add an 'Add Question' button that opens a modal window with fields for question image URL, four option image URLs, and a correct option selector (radio/tick); on submit, save via addQuestion and refresh the question gallery.
Navbar should only show Home, Dashboard, and Admin (admin-only) links. Remove My Results and Leaderboard from navbar.
First user to login is automatically designated as admin; only that principal sees the Admin Panel link. After confirming admin access works, proceed to build out the full Admin Panel structure.

In the Motoko backend (backend/main.mo), ensure the admin designation logic works as follows: the very first principal to successfully authenticate and trigger any backend call (or explicitly the first call to a designated 'claimAdmin' or first admin-gated function) is permanently stored as the admin principal in stable storage. All subsequent calls to admin-only functions from a different principal must be rejected. The admin principal must persist across canister upgrades.

Revert questions and options from text-based back to image-based: restore image URL fields (questionImageUrl, optionImageUrls) in the backend data model and all related functions. In the Admin Panel Question Gallery, replace text input fields with image URL inputs and display question/option images in the gallery. On the Test Page and answer review section, render question and option images instead of text. Image containers must be responsive and adjustable so images are clearly visible on mobile and desktop.

In the Motoko backend (backend/main.mo), revert the Question data model back to image-based fields: replace any plain-text question/option fields with `questionImageUrl: Text` and `optionImageUrls: [Text]` (array of 4 image URLs). Update all related backend functions — addQuestion, getAllQuestions, getTestQuestions — to use these image URL fields. Ensure the data model is consistent with the original image-based schema.

Write a migration module (backend/migration.mo) that transforms any existing questions stored with plain-text fields to the image-URL-based schema (initializing image URL fields as empty strings if needed), so stable state is preserved across the canister upgrade.

Replace image URL input fields in the Admin Panel Add Question modal with direct image upload inputs (file picker from device gallery). Store uploaded images (e.g. as base64 or asset uploads) in the backend. Question and option image containers must be adjustable/resizable and clearly display images on both mobile and desktop.

Update the Motoko backend (backend/main.mo) to store question images and option images as Blob/binary data (Base64-encoded bytes) rather than URL strings. Update the Question data model to use `questionImageData: Text` (base64 data URI) and `optionImageData: [Text]` (array of 4 base64 data URIs). Update addQuestion, getAllQuestions, and getTestQuestions to accept and return base64 image data. Write a migration in backend/migration.mo that maps existing questionImageUrl/optionImageUrls fields to the new base64 fields (initializing empty strings if no prior data exists).

Modify Test Screen UI: each question shows one single image (w-full, object-contain, max-height ~70-75vh on mobile, scrollable, centered, white bg container) containing the full question and all 4 options. Remove separate A/B/C/D option image blocks. Below the image, show 4 full-width text buttons (A, B, C, D, min-height 48-56px) for answer selection with clear selected state (border highlight + background change). Timer, navigation, and submission logic unchanged. Fully responsive (mobile priority).

In the Admin Panel Add Question modal, remove the four option image upload/input fields (Option 1–4 image pickers). The question creation form should only have a single image upload for the combined question+options image. All other logic (correct option selector A/B/C/D, submit, preview) remains unchanged.

In frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, verify that when the user logs in via Internet Identity, the authenticated actor is created and cached correctly with the new identity, and that React Query is notified to invalidate or refetch queries that depend on the authenticated actor (including `isCallerAdmin`). If query invalidation is not triggered after login, add an explicit `queryClient.invalidateQueries` call targeting the admin check query key upon identity change.

In frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, ensure that when the Internet Identity login completes and a new authenticated identity is set, React Query explicitly invalidates all queries (or at minimum the isCallerAdmin query key) so that the admin status check re-runs immediately after login without requiring a page refresh.

In frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, after Internet Identity login completes and a new authenticated identity is set, explicitly call queryClient.invalidateQueries (or invalidateQueries with the isCallerAdmin query key) so that the admin status check re-runs immediately after login without requiring a page refresh. Ensure the authenticated actor is created and cached correctly with the new identity before the query is invalidated.

In frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, after Internet Identity login completes and a new authenticated identity is set, explicitly call queryClient.invalidateQueries targeting the ['isCallerAdmin'] query key so that the admin status check re-runs immediately after login without requiring a page refresh. Ensure the authenticated actor is created and cached with the new identity before the invalidation call.

In frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, after Internet Identity login completes and a new authenticated identity is set, explicitly call queryClient.invalidateQueries targeting the ['isCallerAdmin'] query key so that the admin status check re-runs immediately after login without requiring a page refresh. Ensure the authenticated actor is created and cached with the new identity before the invalidation call.

In frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, after Internet Identity login completes and a new authenticated identity is set, explicitly call queryClient.invalidateQueries targeting all queries (or at minimum ['isCallerAdmin'] and ['actor']) so that the admin status check re-runs immediately after login without requiring a page refresh. Use a useEffect that watches the identity object and triggers invalidation whenever the identity changes from null to a non-null value.

On the Test Screen, make the question image container full-screen (or near full-screen) height so the combined question+options image is easy to see on mobile. Below the image, show only small A, B, C, D option buttons for correct answer selection (no other content between image and buttons).

In frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, add a useEffect that watches the identity object. Whenever identity changes from null/undefined to a non-null value (i.e. the user has just logged in), call queryClient.invalidateQueries() with no filter (invalidate all queries) so that isCallerAdmin and any other identity-dependent queries are immediately re-fetched after login completes.

Fix navbar so Home, Dashboard, Admin Panel (admin only), and Login/Logout buttons are always clearly visible and never hidden; resolve intermittent button/link visibility issue in both desktop and mobile navbar.

In frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts, ensure the useIsCallerAdmin query: (1) is enabled only when identity is non-null, (2) uses a fresh authenticated actor created directly with the identity (not a cached anonymous actor) to call the backend isCallerAdmin() function, (3) uses ['isCallerAdmin', principalString] as the query key so it re-fetches whenever the logged-in principal changes, and (4) has staleTime: 0 and gcTime: 0 so it never returns a stale cached result. This ensures the Admin Panel link appears reliably after login without a page refresh.

In frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, add or verify a useEffect that watches the identity object and, whenever the identity changes from null/undefined to a non-null value (user has just logged in), calls queryClient.invalidateQueries() to invalidate all queries (including isCallerAdmin) so they immediately re-fetch with the new authenticated identity. This prevents the navbar from showing stale unauthenticated state after login.
